  locator: "Section 8 DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSES (eADA test performance)"
  summary: "Erythrocyte adenosine deaminase (eADA) activity helps differentiate DBA syndrome from other anaemias/IBMFS with 84% sensitivity and 95% specificity, but must be measured pre-transfusion and a normal value does not rule out DBA."

- evidence_id: ev_004
  locator: "Section 9 > Chelation therapy > Evaluation of iron overload (cardiac T2*)"
  summary: "Cardiac iron by MRI T2* should stay as close to normal as possible with an acceptable range of 20-35 msec; T2* <20 ms associates with decreased cardiac function/worse survival and <10 ms with imminent heart-failure risk."
  quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d61b2080e875ffca83be9f4aaf4a706616a6f8437f15b76e2244a994988d0406]"
  supports_potential_claims: [clm_pending]
  pediatric_cds:
    population: "Chronically transfused DBA syndrome patients (cardiac MRI iron assessment recommended by age 5 years at latest, repeated yearly)"
    assay_method: "Cardiac MRI T2* (msec); method-specific. Panel notes healthy-volunteer mean T2* >40 ms considered normal; some panelists argued for stricter T2* >25 ms (no consensus)."

    classification: source_supported_fact

---
# Source Card: Diagnosis, treatment, and surveillance of Diamond-Blackfan anaemia syndrome: international consensus statement

## Summary
The 2024 international consensus (53-expert panel, 27 countries; Lancet Haematol 2024;11(5):e368-e382) redefines Diamond-Blackfan anaemia as "DBA syndrome" and sets standardized diagnostic, treatment, and surveillance parameters. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies several numeric reference values with explicit population scope and (where relevant) assay-method dependence: the haemoglobin target (>=9-10 g/dL, age-independent), eADA diagnostic test performance, MRI-based iron-overload targets (LIC and cardiac T2*), the lowered prednisone maintenance ceiling, and chelation-start triggers. The panel is explicit that all recommendations rest on level C evidence.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Hb target >=9-10 g/dL independent of age — locator: Section 9 opening — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:817fffeed062b1372f1ef60d2e87f4be0991f37c95f191564b3b18ae5c74bf15]"
- (ev_002) eADA 84% sensitivity / 95% specificity, pre-transfusion — locator: Section 8 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:46d4f16b59c70d5734949eef6bd90e4f15f1b2445d0053e39485f64fef3cfec1]"
- (ev_003) Target LIC <3 mg Fe/g dry weight (MRI T2*/R2) — locator: Section 9 Evaluation of iron overload — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:188b00a5511b207bc4e4877788825d5baa1310b0a416374aa0ce5f1365b686b4]"
- (ev_004) Cardiac T2* acceptable 20-35 ms (<20 adverse, <10 imminent HF) — locator: Section 9 Evaluation of iron overload — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c39c5a034e55a9e4d30b2917b58efb43960d2b5d16e25d8f0ad25122c8bdfb0d]"; "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:04e6db3d0cbe3384076f72243b39980be340045b52f520510e03816d6cb3a978]"; "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:05a9ed7c1f5ce9e46a3755cfebc7dd27322c10eb3ab71af0b718a215f4526f28]"
- (ev_005) Prednisone maintenance ceiling <=0.3 mg/kg/day (discontinue if Hb>=9 not sustainable at that dose) — locator: Section 9 Oral steroids — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ccee288ef3cee8c10abd2b2e21e22843daa6a004e3fc4838d2bbea59cb5a73bc]"
- (ev_006) Chelation start: ~10 transfusions OR ferritin >500 ng/mL OR TSAT >60% OR elevated LIC — locator: Section 9 Practical considerations — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:60287572f0d7cd6012f7b1fbe441a6176576211dbf3e791cc2d04c733e125f05]"
- (ev_007) 90% symptomatic in first year of life, median age 3 months — locator: Section 4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:14de6c3dcd7e37dfdb732a04369a37c918816e10926f058efc868f4035edbaad]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: "DBA syndrome" of all ages; the Hb target is explicitly age-independent, a change from the 2008 guidance. Onset is overwhelmingly pediatric (median 3 months) but the diagnosis can occur into the sixth decade.
- Assay/method dependence: iron-overload targets are MRI-method-specific — LIC by T2* or R2/FerriScan, cardiac by T2* (msec). Serum ferritin is explicitly stated to be NOT a reliable indicator of iron overload in DBA. eADA MUST be measured pre-transfusion.
- Lifecycle: 2024 consensus, replaces the 2008 DBA consensus; version = "2024 consensus". Prednisone maintenance ceiling and the age-independent Hb target are the notable supersedes.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Cardiac T2* <10 ms is flagged as associated with imminent risk of heart failure (a red-flag / escalation signal). eADA is positioned as a differentiating test but a normal value does NOT rule out DBA (do not use as an exclusion gate). Significant CBC change triggers bone-marrow examination for MDS/AML. These are surveillance/referral signals, not executable actions — the CDS converter owns any action mapping.

## Limitations & conflicts
- All recommendations are level C evidence (panel states no level A/B evidence exists for DBA); thresholds are expert-consensus, not trial-derived.
- Non-consensus alternates preserved, not collapsed: some panelists proposed a stricter prednisone maintenance ceiling of 0.2 mg/kg/day and a stricter cardiac T2* >25 ms — both explicitly WITHOUT consensus; do not average against the consensus 0.3 mg/kg/day and 20-35 ms values.
- Reticulocyte response threshold ("significant reticulocytosis") appears in the source as ">=50-100x10/L" with the standard 10^9/L exponent lost in the extracted text; the exact power-of-ten is not verbatim-locatable in this rendering, so the reticulocyte-response cutoff is recorded as a GAP and NOT asserted here.
- Initial prednisone dose (2 mg/kg/day, max 80 mg/day) and adult maintenance ceiling (10-15 mg/day) are present in the source but are dosing directives; captured only as reported guideline facts, not as executable rules.
- Some target values differ by units in-source (g/dL vs g/L; mg Fe/g dry weight) — preserved verbatim, not normalized.
